enclosed_documents: Forensic Causation Report with annotated photography
summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 4854 NW Homestead Terrace, Riverside, MO 64150, USA. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

code_violations.requirement: Asphalt shingles shall be installed in accordance with this section AND the manufacturer's installation instructions — adopts manufacturer spec as code-enforceable requirement
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer spec controls fastener count, exposure, starter, drip edge, and warranty-critical details

code_violations.requirement: Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — ASTM D226 / D4869 / D6757 labeling required

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis of 11 documented findings, the property at 4854 NW Homestead Terrace, Riverside, MO 64150, USA sustained confirmed storm damage from the March 10, 2026 storm event. Chalk-tested hail impacts across multiple roof slopes reveal consistent mat bruising with embedded granules and fracture patterns at impact sites, confirming hail velocity exceeded the TAMKO architectural shingle impact resistance threshold. Test square documentation of 10+ wind-damaged shingles on a single slope, combined with mechanical seal strip separation on shingle back sides, establishes that both hail and wind mechanisms acted concurrently across the entire roof system. The uniform distribution of damage — confirmed through wide-angle overview photography showing chalk markings across all visible slopes — rules out isolated wear or pre-existing deterioration as causative factors.

The confirmed functional damage to the TAMKO architectural laminated composition shingle roof system and associated components requires full system replacement rather than spot repair. Mixing new TAMKO product with storm-compromised existing shingles that exhibit widespread mat fracture and seal strip failure will not restore functional weatherproofing integrity, as damaged shingles have lost their designed impact resistance and wind uplift adhesion. Furthermore, the IRC as adopted in Missouri requires that roof covering repairs restore the roof to a weather-resistant condition; given that damage is distributed uniformly across all documented slopes with both hail and wind mechanisms confirmed, isolated repairs cannot satisfy this performance standard.

Based on the documented damage, applicable building codes under the MO-IRC, and recognized industry standards, our analysis identifies that full replacement of the roof covering system — including all TAMKO architectural laminated composition shingles, underlayment, and compromised flashings or accessories — is warranted. The forensic evidence supports this determination: the approximately 12-year-old roof system (installed circa 2014) sustained storm damage that is systemic in nature, rendering partial replacement technically infeasible without compromising the weatherproofing performance of the entire assembly. This scope recommendation is grounded solely in the physical evidence documented at the property and the functional damage confirmed through field testing.

This forensic determination was conducted in accordance with HAAG Engineering residential roof inspection standards for identifying functional hail and wind damage, NRCA guidelines for evaluating roof system repairability and replacement thresholds, and the International Residential Code (IRC) as adopted in Missouri governing roof covering performance requirements. The classification of mat bruising with embedded granules as functional damage — rather than cosmetic — is consistent with HAAG's published criteria distinguishing damage that compromises shingle service life from superficial surface marks.
forensic_findings.damage_summary: TAMKO architectural asphalt shingles exhibit combined hail and wind damage from the March 10, 2026 storm event. Front-side shingle sample shows severe granule displacement with exposed and bruised asphalt mat — functional damage confirmed via close-up inspection. The shingle back side confirms TAMKO-Joplin manufacture with compromised seal strip adhesion indicating mechanical wind separation. Macro close-ups reveal embedded granules and mat fracture at impact sites, confirming hail velocity sufficient to cause functional damage. The full roof overview shows widespread color variation and granule loss across the entire roof plane, consistent with a combined hail and wind event. Full replacement is warranted — spot repair is infeasible due to uniform damage distribution across all visible slopes. Combined hail and wind damage documented across asphalt shingle roof. Chalk-circled hail impacts on field shingles confirm mat bruising with functional damage. Test square notation 'W=10+' indicates 10+ wind-damaged shingles on one slope. Satellite dish/vent cap shows chalk-tested hail dents. Wide-angle overview reveals extensive chalk markings across multiple slopes with numerous confirmed impact points. Full replacement warranted — damage is distributed across all visible slopes with both hail and wind mechanisms confirmed.

executive_summary: On March 10, 2026, the property located at 4854 NW Homestead Terrace, Riverside, MO 64150, USA sustained damage from a combined hail and wind storm event. The roofing system consists of TAMKO architectural laminated composite shingles, confirmed via manufacturer markings on the back side of extracted field samples. A forensic inspection of the roof and associated components was conducted, with 10 photographs documenting damage conditions across all visible roof slopes.

Field inspection confirmed widespread hail and wind damage distributed across the entire roof system. Chalk-circle testing of field shingles identified numerous hail impacts exhibiting severe granule displacement, exposed asphalt mat, and subsurface mat bruising — consistent with hail of sufficient velocity to cause functional damage. Macro close-up examination further revealed embedded granule fracture and mat cracking at impact sites. Wind damage was simultaneously documented, with test-square analysis indicating ten or more mechanically separated shingles on a single slope, corroborated by compromised seal-strip adhesion observed on extracted sample backs.

Full roof system replacement is warranted based on the uniform distribution of both hail and wind damage across all inspected slopes, rendering selective spot repair technically infeasible. Isolated repairs cannot restore the compromised granule-to-mat bond or re-establish factory seal-strip adhesion on wind-lifted shingles, and replacement shingles cannot be color- or profile-matched to the weathered existing field due to manufacturing lot variation and in-service granule erosion. Per IRC requirements as adopted in Missouri, roofing repairs must restore the system to a watertight, code-compliant condition — a standard that cannot be met through patchwork remediation of a uniformly damaged roof plane.
key_arguments: TAMKO architectural shingles show bruised mat with embedded granules at circular hail impact sites — functional damage per HAAG standard requiring full replacement
Seal strip on shingle back side shows clean mechanical separation consistent with wind uplift forces during the March 10, 2026 storm event
Macro close-ups confirm mat fracture — shingles are soft to the touch at impact points, proving hail velocity exceeded shingle impact resistance threshold
Full roof overview reveals uniform granule loss and color variation across all courses on the visible slope, ruling out isolated wear and confirming storm-wide hail damage pattern
Combined hail and wind damage renders spot repair infeasible — mixing new TAMKO product with storm-compromised existing shingles will not restore functional weatherproofing
Test square notation 'W=10+' confirms 10+ wind-damaged shingles on documented slope — exceeds repair threshold
Chalk-circled hail impacts on field shingles show functional mat damage requiring full replacement
Satellite dish/vent cap chalk test confirms hail dent pattern on soft metal component
Wide-angle overview reveals chalk markings distributed across entire roof slope — damage is not isolated
Combined hail and wind mechanisms present — directional wind damage alongside uniform hail impacts confirms March 10, 2026 storm event
Estimated roof age: ~12 years (installed circa 2014) — Shingle Type: Laminate/Architectural shingle

code_citation.jurisdiction: Missouri — no statewide residential code; most jurisdictions adopt IRC 2018 or 2021

manufacturer_specs.requirement: 4-6 nails per shingle, ProStart starter required at eaves and rakes
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: GAF System Plus/Silver Pledge warranty VOID without proper starter and nailing pattern

code_citation.requirement: Existing roofing materials shall be removed before applying new roofing when existing roof has two or more applications of any type of roof covering
code_citation.supplement_argument: Complete tear-off is required per code for a code-compliant installation. Overlay is not code-compliant when damage necessitates deck inspection.
code_citation.title: Reroofing — Tear-Off Requirement

line_items.supplement_argument: Complete tear-off is required per code for a code-compliant installation. Overlay is not code-compliant when damage necessitates deck inspection.

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Functional damage (mat fracture, granule loss exposing substrate) = full replacement
Mat fracture from hail impact = functional damage requiring replacement

manufacturer_specs.document: Detecting Hail Damage to Asphalt Shingles
manufacturer_specs.requirement: Systematic hail damage assessment criteria for functional vs cosmetic

code_citation.requirement: Roofing shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions. Manufacturer instructions carry the force of building code.
code_citation.supplement_argument: Per R905.1, manufacturer installation instructions are legally binding building code. All components specified by the manufacturer (starter, underlayment, flashing) are code-required, not optional upgrades.
code_citation.title: Manufacturer Installation Instructions = Law

line_items.description: Laminated comp shingle roofing - w/out felt
line_items.supplement_argument: Per R905.1, manufacturer installation instructions are legally binding building code. All components specified by the manufacturer (starter, underlayment, flashing) are code-required, not optional upgrades.

code_citation.jurisdiction: Missouri — no statewide residential code; most jurisdictions adopt IRC 2018 or 2021

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Required at eaves, valleys, and around penetrations per GAF system requirements
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: GAF warranty VOID without WeatherWatch/StormGuard at eaves and valleys

code_citation.requirement: Ice barrier required at eaves extending from eave edge to at least 24 inches past interior wall line, and in valleys
code_citation.supplement_argument: Ice & water barrier is required by code at all eaves and valleys. This is not an optional upgrade — it is a mandatory building code requirement for a code-compliant installation.
code_citation.title: Ice Barrier Requirement

line_items.description: Underlayment - felt 15# (deck area not covered by I&W)
line_items.supplement_argument: Ice & water barrier is required by code at all eaves and valleys. This is not an optional upgrade — it is a mandatory building code requirement for a code-compliant installation.
8 hours roofer labor mandatory on every roofing estimate per industry standard

photo_annotations.p03_01: PRIMARY: HAIL. Severe granule displacement exposes bruised mat — functional damage confirmed on TAMKO architectural shingle.
photo_annotations.p03_02: PRIMARY: WIND. Seal strip mechanically separated; TAMKO-Joplin back stamp confirms product — wind broke adhesion.
photo_annotations.p03_03: PRIMARY: HAIL. Macro view shows embedded granules and mat fracture at circular impact site — soft to the touch.
photo_annotations.p04_01: PRIMARY: HAIL. Close-up reveals circular depression with granule loss exposing asphalt — functional hail damage.
photo_annotations.p04_02: PRIMARY: HAIL. Full roof overview shows widespread granule loss across all courses — uniform hail pattern, no directional wind bias.
photo_annotations.p04_03: Chalk circle marks hail impact on field shingle — bruised mat soft to the touch, functional damage confirmed.
photo_annotations.p05_01: Test square notation reads 'W=10+' — 10+ wind-damaged shingles confirmed on this slope.
photo_annotations.p05_02: Chalk circle marks hail strike on field shingle — circular depression with embedded granules confirms impact.
photo_annotations.p05_03: Chalk test on satellite dish/vent cap reveals gaps in chalk line — confirmed hail dents on soft metal.
photo_annotations.p06_01: Overview of roof slope shows extensive chalk marks across field — multiple hail and wind damage points documented.

photo_integrity.stamp: PHOTO INTEGRITY VERIFIED — 100% | 10 photos analyzed, 0 flagged for manipulation indicators | DumbRoof.AI Fraud Detection Engine
photo_integrity.summary: All 10 photos show authentic storm damage inspection documentation with no fraud indicators detected. Photos include retained shingle samples with manufacturer identification (TAMKO Joplin), macro-level hail impact documentation showing natural granule displacement patterns, standard chalk marking techniques on both shingles and metal components, and test square hit counts. Shadows, lighting, and perspective are consistent across all on-roof photos. No evidence of staged damage, photo manipulation, digital editing artifacts, or property misrepresentation.

weather.storm_description: Searched 2026-03-10 ± 2 days for storm events near property. Near the subject property: 4.5" hail reported 6.3 mi from property. Hail reports in surrounding area: SPC Report — 1 NE Weatherby Lake, MO (4.5"), SPC Report — 2 SW Beverly Hills, KS (3.0"), NEXRAD KEAX (2.75"), NEXRAD KMCI (2.5"), NEXRAD KTWX (2.25"), SPC Report — 3 ENE Lake Waukomis, MO (2.0"), SPC Report — 5 NE Lake Waukomis, MO (2.0"), NEXRAD KSGF (2.0").
